decks.notes: The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient, cost-effective creatures and pump spells. By utilizing cards like Elspeth, Storm Slayer, Practiced Offense, and Leatherhead, Swamp Stalker, the deck aims to quickly establish a dominant board presence and deal significant damage early in the game. With a focus on building a strong creature base with cards like Badgermole Cub and Botanical Brawler, supported by ramp with Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, this deck aims to apply consistent pressure and close out the game swiftly.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy by using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game. It also utilizes creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure and provide additional value. The mana base is designed to support a balance between black and green spells, with lands like Overgrown Tomb and Blooming Marsh. Overall, the deck aims to outlast opponents through a combination of removal, disruption, and powerful creatures.

The primary focus of this Dimir Aggro deck is to quickly apply pressure on the opponent by playing aggressive creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, Floodpits Drowner, and Moon-Circuit Hacker. The deck also utilizes disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Go for the Throat to control the opponent's board and protect its own creatures. With a mix of evasive threats and removal spells, the deck aims to maintain a steady assault on the opponent while disrupting their game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with a combination of aggression and control.

The main focus of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to swarm the board with low-cost creatures and use pump spells to deal massive damage to the opponent quickly. By utilizing efficient creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to ramp up mana, combined with powerful cards like Practiced Offense and Surrak, Elusive Hunter, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to establish their game plan. The inclusion of removal spells like Skyclave Apparition and protection lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire help to ensure the deck's aggressive strategy is successful.

This deck focuses on assembling an explosive combo using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate massive value and overwhelm the opponent. It includes various support cards like Formidable Speaker, Bitter Triumph, and Collective Brutality to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also utilizes mana-fixing lands like Temple Garden and Concealed Courtyard to ensure a smooth mana base for executing its combo. With a mix of ramp, removal, and combo pieces, this deck aims to quickly set up its win condition and dominate the battlefield.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, as well as hand disruption with Thoughtseize. It also aims to control the board with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck includes powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to close out the game. It also utilizes lands like Mutavault and Boseiju, Who Endures for additional utility. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent in a grindy game of attrition.

The deck focuses on using control elements such as removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, along with hand disruption like Thoughtseize, to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sentinel of the Nameless City to apply pressure and close out the game. The deck aims to grind out advantage over time and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful threats and disruption, ultimately controlling the pace of the game and coming out on top.

The deck focuses on utilizing a variety of control spells and abilities to disrupt opponent's strategies and maintain board control. It includes cards that allow for card draw, counter spells, mana acceleration, and removal. The deck also features powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria to generate card advantage and control the game. Additionally, it includes cards like Beza, the Bounding Spring and Ultima to provide alternative win conditions. The mana base is designed to support the control strategy and provide consistency in casting spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing evasive creatures and disruption spells to quickly pressure the opponent and control the board. Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker provide early aggression, while cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize disrupt the opponent's game plan. Mutavault and Mutavault, along with the Dimir color combination, allow for a versatile and adaptable playstyle. Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provide powerful finishers to close out the game. Overall, the deck aims to be fast and efficient, overwhelming opponents with a mix of aggression and control.

This deck focuses on playing aggressively with a mix of faeries and control elements. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also deploying threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Moon-Circuit Hacker to apply pressure. Mutavault and Gloomlake Verge provide additional creature support, while Sheoldred, the Apocalypse serves as a powerful late-game threat. The deck aims to control the board and chip away at the opponent's life total, ultimately overwhelming them with a combination of disruptive spells and evasive creatures.

This deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with spells in order to bring Arclight Phoenix back from the graveyard to attack the opponent. The deck includes a variety of low-cost spells and card draw effects to enable this strategy. The key goal is to cast multiple spells in a turn to bring back Arclight Phoenix and deal significant damage to the opponent, ultimately overwhelming them with a fast and aggressive gameplay style.

The primary focus of this deck is to utilize the Lotus Field card in combination with other cards such as Lumra, Scapeshift, and The Wandering Minstrel to create a powerful combo that generates a large amount of mana. By ramping up mana quickly, the deck aims to cast high impact spells and overwhelm opponents with its explosive plays. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Thassa's Oracle and Marang River Regent for alternate win conditions, providing flexibility in its strategy. Overall, the key concept revolves around efficient mana generation and executing game-winning combos.

This deck focuses on utilizing a companion card, Yorion, Sky Nomad, to provide additional value and control options. It includes a variety of spells and creatures to control the game by bouncing, destroying, and manipulating opponents' resources. The deck also includes various land cards to ensure consistent mana production. By utilizing powerful control tools like Narset, Parter of Veils and Fatal Push, along with versatile spells like Nowhere to Run and Stormchaser's Talent, this Dimir Control deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents in a Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this Red Deck Wins aggro deck is to rapidly deploy low-cost creatures and burn spells to deal damage to the opponent quickly and efficiently. By utilizing creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage alongside burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game and close out the match before the opponent can stabilize. The inclusion of utility lands like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional reach and versatility to the deck's aggressive game plan.

The main concept of this deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with instants and sorceries to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard. This is achieved through cards like Sleight of Hand, Treasure Cruise, and Opt. The deck also includes removal spells like Lightning Axe and Abrade to clear the way for attacking with Arclight Phoenix. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Picklock Prankster and Into the Flood Maw to further accelerate the graveyard filling process. The deck aims to be aggressive and efficient in getting Arclight Phoenix back on the battlefield for quick wins.

The main focus of this aggro deck is to build up devotion to green by playing cards that ramp up mana quickly and efficiently. The deck utilizes cards like The Great Henge, Turntimber Symbiosis, and Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx to generate mana and cast powerful creatures like Elder Gargaroth, Titan of Industry, and Craterhoof Behemoth. Nature's Rhythm and Badgermole Cub provide additional support and removal while cards like Slumbering Trudge and Spider Manifestation contribute to the aggressive playstyle of the deck. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a strong board presence and powerful creatures.
